title: Residual vector-quantized location codebook: hard nearest-neighbor coding replaces the shared-softmax cell field
hypothesis: 15 straight rounds (36-50) have failed to beat champion exp-35's 743.07 m, and the project's own diagnostic probes (champion-failure-mode-probes memory) pin the wall precisely: the true cell is the field's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) on 33-58% of crops in every bucket -- a lookalike cell on the far side of the city stays directly comparable, in raw logit magnitude, to the true cell under ONE shared softmax normalization. Every one of the 15 rounds since the champion attacked this from within that same family and lost: flat-table reparameterizations (39: coordinate-generated field, 1103.28 m; 40: coarse-to-fine field+offset, 1011.58 m; 45: retrieve-then-rerank shortlist, 1213.14 m; 46: factorized row/col axes, gated 1e9), objective swaps on the SAME field (34/36: hardest-impostor margin hinge, twice refuted), trunk-objective swaps feeding the SAME field (41-44: contrastive/reconstruction pretraining, 846.42 m best), and even the two designs that finally left classification-with-a-shared-softmax behind still lost badly: exp 49's dense per-patch consensus (no field, 256 votes attention-averaged into one blended answer) scored 2655.65 m -- worse than the champion by 3.6x, a real, measured refutation of naive soft-averaging as the fix; exp 50's spatial mixture-of-experts (disjoint per-neighborhood classifiers, still classification+softmax at both the coarse and fine stage, just partitioned) scored 1109.13 m. Both of the non-classification attempts still routed every decision through a SHARED, dense, differentiable mechanism -- one shared attention-weighted mean (49) or one shared teacher-forced geographic partition (50) -- so a lookalike's contribution to the final answer was diluted, not structurally excluded. Across all 50 rounds and 7 category values used (architecture/loss/augmentation/relighting/training/other), 'quantization' -- named explicitly in CLAUDE.md SS3 as an open axis and by this round's own patience directive as a legitimate untried pivot family -- has NEVER been the primary driver of a single experiment. This design tests it directly: replace the classifier (or classifier-like field/voting mechanism) entirely with a small, LEARNED, two-stage residual vector-quantization codebook. Location is encoded by hard nearest-neighbor lookup (argmin L2 distance) against a 48-entry coarse codebook, then the residual is looked up again against a second, independent 48-entry fine codebook -- never a shared softmax over more than 48 comparably-scaled candidates at once, and never a soft blend across all candidates the way exp 49's attention-weighted mean was. Because the codebook indices are also graded by the downstream coordinate-regression loss (via a straight-through gradient into the encoder), two geographically distant but visually similar locations that would collide onto the same code pair directly increase training loss for one of them -- concrete gradient pressure to keep them apart, which cross-entropy's 'good enough if the true class has decent relative probability' criterion never applied.
method: In model/model.py: delete the ImageNet/MobileNetV3-Small trunk, the 1024-way loc_logits/dark_logits/gate field, GRID_K/_grid_centers/DECODE_BETA/TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, and the soft-argmax decode entirely. Replace with: (1) a from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->128 ch, stride 2 each, GroupNorm+ReLU, 128x128->8x8x128, no pretrained weights anywhere); (2) a descriptor head (128-d GAP + a 1x1-conv layout squeeze of the 8x8 grid, concatenated, then linearly projected to a 64-d 'address' vector); (3) a two-stage residual VQ codebook -- stage-1 nn.Embedding(48,64), stage-2 nn.Embedding(48,64) applied to the stage-1 residual, both with argmin-L2 hard assignment + straight-through gradient + commitment/codebook losses; (4) a coordinate-lookup decode -- nn.Embedding(48,2) coarse coordinate per stage-1 code + nn.Embedding(48,2) tanh-bounded fine offset per stage-2 code, summed and clamped to [0,1]; (5) a confidence head reading the stage-1 and stage-2 quantization residual norms plus each stage's margin-to-runner-up-code, replacing the old field-shape confidence input. In model/train.py: delete compute_cell_weights/CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA/NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R/crop_cell (GRID_K-based static pixel-appearance table) and replace with a dynamic per-training-location EMA of the model's own live squared coordinate error, recomputed into the sampling distribution at the start of every epoch, blended 0.5/0.5 against uniform (same blend spirit as the retired confusability sampler, now driven by the new architecture's own regression signal instead of a precomputed pixel-similarity table, since there is no cell grid left to compute pixel confusability over).
expected_outcome: If shared-scale comparability (not descriptor content, not classifier capacity) is really the wall the last 15 rounds jointly point at, worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 500-680 m (8-33%), because no two candidates anywhere in the decode are ever ranked against more than 47 others under one shared normalization, and the regression gradient actively penalizes code collisions between distinct locations rather than tolerating them the way cross-entropy did. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: codebook utilization (fraction of the 48x48=2304 code pairs actually used by >1 held-out crop) should be reasonably spread, not collapsed onto a handful of codes (a known VQ pathology); the rate at which two DIFFERENT true locations more than ~500 m apart share the exact same code pair should be low relative to how often the champion's field put the true cell outside its own top-20. Falsification forks: (a) if regression converges cleanly and codebook utilization is healthy but the primary metric doesn't improve, the wall is not candidate-comparability at all -- redirect at raw descriptor information content, a materially new diagnosis after 15 rounds of decode/aggregation churn; (b) if codebook collapse is observed (most crops routing to <10 of the 48 stage-1 codes), this is a distinguishable optimization failure (VQ codebooks are known to collapse without careful init/commitment weighting), arguing for an EMA codebook update or a reset-dead-codes trick next round rather than abandoning quantization; (c) if the metric regresses sharply despite healthy utilization, hard nearest-neighbor lookup with a straight-through estimator is itself too unstable a training signal for this task, which would be new evidence against the quantization family specifically (distinct from every prior failure mode logged, none of which used a non-differentiable hard assignment).
eli5: Instead of asking the model to rank the crop's location against 1,024 possible spots on one shared scoreboard -- which is exactly why a lookalike neighborhood clear across town keeps beating the real one -- we give it two small 'which of these 48 does this look most like' filing steps instead, like sorting mail into a coarse bin first and then a fine sub-bin, each bin owning its own rough map position. Nothing ever has to out-rank all 1,024 rivals at once anymore, and every filing decision is graded directly by how close the final guessed spot lands to the truth, so mixing up two look-alike but far-apart places now directly costs the model accuracy instead of being a shrug-worthy near-miss.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop and its three exact 90-degree pixel rotations all travel through the same trunk/codebook/decode side by side; instead of averaging field logits before the decode (the old mechanism), each of the 4 turns now produces its own complete quantized coordinate decode, and the 4 decodes are combined by a residual-confidence-weighted average -- a genuine vote between finished answers, not a pre-decode logit blend

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each, GroupNorm+ReLU), 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no pretrained weights, no torchvision backbone of any kind

stages.name: Descriptor
stages.detail: 128-number texture average (GAP) plus a 1x1-conv layout squeeze of the 8x8 feature grid, concatenated then linearly projected down to a compact 64-number 'address' vector -- this compact address is what gets looked up in the new codebook below, instead of feeding a giant per-cell classifier directly

stages.name: Location codebook (two-stage residual lookup)
stages.detail: the 64-number address is matched, by plain nearest-neighbor distance, against a small learned table of 48 'coarse place' entries, picking whichever is closest; the leftover difference between the address and its chosen coarse entry is matched again against a second, independent table of 48 'fine correction' entries -- no classifier, no shared 1024-way scoreboard anywhere, just two small, independent nearest-neighbor picks, each ranked only against its own 47 alternatives

stages.name: Coordinate lookup decode
stages.detail: each of the 48 coarse entries owns its own learned rough (u, v) guess, and each of the 48 fine entries owns its own small learned nudge (bounded to a modest radius); the final position is the coarse guess plus the fine nudge for whichever two entries were actually picked -- replaces the old sharpen-and-average-over-1024-candidates soft-argmax entirely

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads how far the crop's own address vector actually sits from its two chosen table entries at each lookup stage (a bad match = large leftover distance = a genuine 'this doesn't look like anywhere I know well' signal), plus the gap to each stage's runner-up entry, through a small calibrated hit predictor with the same post-hoc per-bucket keep-floor shift as before

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the static, precomputed pixel-appearance confusability table (exp 35, built once from reference.tif's per-cell color/texture, keyed to a now-deleted fixed grid) is replaced by a dynamic per-training-location EMA of the model's own current squared coordinate regression error, recomputed into the epoch sampling distribution (blended 0.5 error-weighted / 0.5 uniform) at the start of every epoch -- a training-data mechanism native to a model that now scores locations by direct regression error rather than by grid-cell membership

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: Huber loss on the final decoded (u, v) (flowing back through the straight-through estimator into the encoder and both coordinate-embedding tables) + commitment and codebook losses at both VQ stages (push the encoder's address toward its chosen codes and vice versa) + confidence BCE against the same GOOD_ERR_UV hit definition as before -- no cross-entropy, no probability field, no shared softmax anywhere in the loss

implementation_brief: model/model.py -- DELETE: `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`/`IMAGENET_STD` + the norm_mean/norm_std buffers, `_build_pretrained_trunk`, `GRID_K`, `TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS`, `DECODE_BETA`, `_grid_centers`, and every field-related member of `TinyLocNet` (`layout_squeeze`'s old 1024-wide use, `loc_logits`, `dark_logits`, `gate`, `cell_u`/`cell_v` buffers). Do not import mobilenet_v3_small or reference `mnv3s_features8.pt` anywhere in model/ (backbonecheck.py strips comments/docstrings via AST so prose mentions in the history log at the top of the file are fine, but no executable import or path string may name it). ADD: a from-scratch feature extractor (4x `nn.Sequential(Conv2d(stride=2), GroupNorm, ReLU)`, channels 3->32->64->96->128, 128x128->8x8x128 -- verify with a zero-tensor probe shape as the current code already does); a descriptor head (GAP over the 8x8x128 map -> 128-d; a `nn.Conv2d(128, 8, 1)` layout squeeze flattened to 512-d; concat to 640-d; `nn.Linear(640, 64)` projection -> the 'address' vector `e`, per rotation copy); two `nn.Embedding(48, 64)` codebooks (`codebook1`, `codebook2`); a lookup function computing pairwise L2 distance from `e` (and later the residual) to each codebook's 48 rows (`torch.cdist` or manual expand/sub/pow/sum -- verify whichever form traces cleanly under `torch.onnx.export(..., opset_version=17, dynamo=False)`; fall back to the manual form if `cdist` doesn't export), `argmin` for the hard index, `codebook.weight[idx]` gather for the quantized vector, and the standard VQ straight-through trick (`q_st = e + (q - e).detach()`) so gradient flows to the encoder as identity while a separate commitment loss `((q.detach() - e) ** 2).mean()` and codebook loss `((q - e.detach()) ** 2).mean()` (weight ~0.25 each, both stages, matching common VQ-VAE defaults) train the codebook itself; residual for stage 2 is `e - q1` (using the plain, non-straight-through `q1`) fed into the identical lookup against `codebook2`. Two more `nn.Embedding(48, 2)` tables (`coarse_coord`, init `nn.init.uniform_(weight, 0, 1)` so early training starts inside the valid coordinate range rather than default N(0,1)) and (`fine_offset`, small init, its output passed through `torch.tanh(...) * OFFSET_SCALE` with `OFFSET_SCALE = 0.06` as a new module constant) produce the decoded `u, v = clamp(coarse_coord[idx1] + fine_offset[idx2], 0, 1)`. Confidence head: small MLP over `[dist_to_q1, margin1 (2nd-nearest - nearest at stage 1), dist_to_q2, margin2, address-pooled-over-rotations]` -> sigmoid, keep the existing `conf_shift` buffer and calibration contract unchanged. Rotation handling: run the whole address->lookup->decode pipeline independently for each of the 4 `_c4_stack` copies (keep `_c4_stack` itself verbatim), then combine the 4 per-turn `(u, v)` decodes into one final estimate weighted by each turn's own `1 / (dist_to_q1 + dist_to_q2 + eps)` (more confident turns count more), and separately average the 4 turns' confidence-head inputs (or just their final z-logits) the way the champion averaged field logits. `forward(x, return_logits=...)` should be renamed to something like `forward(x, return_aux=False)` returning `(out, aux_dict)` where `aux_dict` carries whatever `loss_fn` needs (`e`, `q1_st`, `q1`, `q2_st`, `q2`, `idx1`, `idx2` per rotation or already-pooled) -- this is an internal model.py<->train.py contract, not the frozen ONNX export contract, so it is free to change; keep `export_onnx`'s signature and the `[u, v, conf]` output shape/range exactly as documented in the module docstring's contract paragraph (restate it verbatim at the top of the rewritten file). `loss_fn(pred, aux, target_uv)` -- replace the CE term entirely with `nn.functional.huber_loss(pred[:, :2], target_uv)` for the coordinate term (or keep the L2 form if simpler -- either is fine, just no field/CE anywhere), add the four commitment/codebook loss terms (stage 1 and 2), and keep the confidence BCE term with the same `GOOD_ERR_UV`-based 'good' label logic as today. model/train.py -- DELETE `compute_cell_weights`, `CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA`, `NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R`, the `crop_cell`/`cell_w`/`raw_w`/`weighted` block in `train_area`, and the `GRID_K` import (no longer exported by model.py). ADD: a persistent `loc_err_ema = np.ones(len(crops))` array (module-level default `ERROR_EMA_DECAY = 0.9`, `ERROR_SAMPLER_ALPHA = 0.5`) initialized before the epoch loop; `sample_epoch` must additionally return a parallel `idxs` array of each drawn sample's index into `crops` (it already iterates `for i in part: c = crops[i]` -- just also append `i` to an `idxs` list and return it alongside `x, y`); at the start of every epoch, recompute `crop_probs = ERROR_SAMPLER_ALPHA * (loc_err_ema / loc_err_ema.sum()) + (1 - ERROR_SAMPLER_ALPHA) * uniform` from the CURRENT `loc_err_ema` state before calling `sample_epoch`; inside the minibatch loop, after `out, aux = model(xb, return_aux=True)`, compute `err = ((out[:, :2] - yb) ** 2).sum(dim=1).detach().cpu().numpy()` and update `loc_err_ema[idx_batch] = ERROR_EMA_DECAY * loc_err_ema[idx_batch] + (1 - ERROR_EMA_DECAY) * err` where `idx_batch` is `idxs[perm[i:i+64].numpy()]` (map the permuted minibatch slice back through the epoch's `idxs` array). Log `codebook1_utilization`/`codebook2_utilization` (fraction of the 48 codes used at least once over the epoch's draws) into the returned `info` dict alongside the existing calibration fields, since the pre-registered falsification forks depend on it being inspectable post-hoc. Everything else in train.py (the outer CLI, `--area`/--out-dir/--epochs/--max-crops-per-bucket/--seed`, `prepare_realizations`, `calibrate_conf_shift`'s external behavior, the ONNX export call, ONNX file naming) is unchanged -- restate for the implementer: `train.py`'s CLI contract and `model/model.py`'s `estimate_position`/ONNX docstring contract (1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1] in, `[[u, v, conf]]` out, u/v normalized map coords, conf in [0,1]) are frozen by the scorer and must not change shape.
